title: round4_phase1_complete
analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 exhaustive counterparty analysis on Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 days 1â€“3 (4281 trades merged to 4281 event rows). Forward moves = K steps along price tape for same (day, product). Aggression = price vs concurrent BBO (aggr_buy / aggr_sell / passive_mid). Script: manual_traders/R4/r4_counterparty_gated/run_r4_phase1_analysis.py

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Mark 67 aggressive buys on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT show strongly positive mean forward mid at K=5,20 (nâ‰ˆ164, tâ‰ˆ13 and 5.8) with ~64â€“84% positive at short horizons; stable positive mean across days 1â€“3 (see r4_mark67_aggr_extract_fwd5_by_day.csv). Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 at K=5 show negative mean fwd (âˆ’0.20, tâ‰ˆâˆ’3.3, n=160) but Mark01â†’Mark22 on VEV_5300 reverses sign by day (day1 mean âˆ’0.70 vs day2 +0.18 in r4_cell_mean_fwd20_by_day_pair_symbol.csv) â€” not day-stable as a simple fade.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pairÃ—symbol cell means + residuals)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Residual file supports second-order checks; dominant Mark01â†’Mark22 cells on wings are near zero on average with day heterogeneity on VEV_5300 â€” no large uniform positive residual after simple cell baseline across all days.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 3) Sequence / graph (directed buyerâ†’seller + 2-hop motifs vs extract fwd20)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Top directed pair Mark01â†’Mark22 matches sketch; two-hop Mark38â†’Mark14â†’Mark38 is frequent (n=160) with negative mean extract fwd20 (âˆ’0.58) while Mark55â†’Mark01â†’Mark22 (n=25) shows small positive mean (+0.16) â€” structural chains exist but need Phase-2 conditioning (burst, symbol) before trading.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 4) Burst structure (same day,timestamp â‰¥4 prints) vs random control
phase1_bullets.conclusion: 293 burst timestamps vs 879 control samples: mean VELVET forward-20-step mid change +0.33 vs +0.11 on control (r4_burst_vs_control_summary.json) â€” bursts associate with higher short-horizon extract drift on this slice.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 5) Adverse selection proxy (aggressive buy: passive seller forward mid)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: Worst mean fwd_mid_20 after aggressive buys hitting Mark 14 as seller on HYDROGEL_PACK (~âˆ’0.35, n=507); other cells less negative â€” candidate avoid-providing-liquidity-to pattern on hydro when Mark14 is passive seller, needs execution sim.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: After Mark 67 aggressive buy on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, short-horizon extract mid drifts up (K=5 mean ~+2.0, tâ‰ˆ13, n=164; day-split means ~1.75â€“2.2 on days 1â€“3).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Mid-only; not yet validated with worse fills or inventory.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Same-timestamp burst (â‰¥4 prints) predicts higher extract forward 20-tick move than random non-burst times (+0.33 vs +0.11).
Mark 22 aggressive sell on VEV_5300 at K=5: negative mean forward mid on the same symbol (âˆ’0.20, tâ‰ˆâˆ’3.3, n=160).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Mark01â†’Mark22 cell splits by day (5300 negative day1, positive day2) â€” use with regime split in Phase 2.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Frequent two-hop Mark38â†’Mark14â†’Mark38 coincides with negative extract fwd20 mean (âˆ’0.58, n=160).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.effect_size: âˆ’0.58 extract mid change 20 steps after second hop timestamp
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Consecutive-trade chain definition is coarse; refine with time windows.

top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.edge: Mark 14 as passive seller on HYDROGEL after aggressive buys shows most negative mean fwd_mid_20 in hydro slice (~âˆ’0.35, n=507).
top_5_tradeable_edges_ranked.caveat: Adverse-selection proxy only; needs quote-level sim.

negative_or_null_findings: Mark 38 aggressive flow on HYDROGEL_PACK shows no significant consistent directional fwd across K (|t|<1 in participant table).
Mark 55 aggressive sells on extract at K=100 are significantly negative (tâ‰ˆâˆ’2.5) but short horizons weaker â€” mixed story, not a clean long signal.
Simple Mark01â†’Mark22 basket on VEV_5300 is not uniformly a fade across days (sign flip day 1 vs 2 in cell table).
Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5500 K=5 are negative but small magnitude (mean âˆ’0.026) â€” economically marginal vs tick size.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 completion gate satisfied: all five Phase-1 bullets have CSV/JSON/txt outputs under manual_traders/R4/r4_counterparty_gated/analysis_outputs/. Proceed to Phase 2 (named-bot conditioning, microstructure, cross-leadâ€“lag) only after strategy code consumes these edges; do not claim 200k/day until backtested.

title: round4_phase2_complete
analysis_description: Phase 2 scripts: run_r4_phase2_analysis.py (burst-conditioned fwd20, signed-flow leadâ€“lag vs extract fwd20, microprice file, spread-tight regime table, M01â†’M22 burst residuals) + run_r4_phase2_build_signals.py (Mark67 keys for live tick). trader_v1 encodes spread-tight + optional Mark67 lift; backtest R4 days 1â€“3.

phase2_bullets.conclusion: Burst-conditioned table shows Mark67â†’Mark22 extract prints have high mean fwd20 when burst_type=no_burst (n=73); Mark67 aggr_buy split burst vs no_burst is inconclusive (only n=2 in burst bucket). Mark01â†’Mark22 burst rows on VEV_5200 show positive mean fwd20 (n=11) while same pair on VEV_5300 negative (n=131) â€” refines Phase-1 pair story: basket burst structure matters by strike.
Extract BBO spread<=2 (tight=1): mean fwd_mid_20 â‰ˆ +1.16 (n=1217) vs tight=0 mean â‰ˆ âˆ’0.056 (n=28723) â€” strong regime split orthogonal to counterparty IDs; trader_v1 uses this as primary gate.
Same-tick signed extract flow vs subsequent extract fwd20 corr â‰ˆ +0.13 at lag 0, weaker at 1â€“5 â€” mild short-horizon momentum co-movement, not a standalone large edge.
Same spread-tight table: signal survives only in tight book regime for forward-mid stats (aligns with R3 Sonic intuition extended to R4 extract tape).
Not run in this pass (no IV column in tape); deferred to Phase 3 or dedicated BS-from-mids pass.
Phase-1 file reused: Mark14 passive seller on hydro after aggr buys remains worst cell â€” trader_v1 does not yet fade hydro vs Mark14 (future rule).
Not implemented in trader_v1 (no inventory model); Phase-2 evidence only.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Extract tight BBO (spread <= 2) associates with much higher mean 20-tick forward mid (+1.16 vs -0.06); encoded as primary regime in trader_v1.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Confirms Phase-1 burst-vs-control (extract drifts) through a simpler always-on microstructure gate not requiring counterparty IDs.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Mark01â†’Mark22 burst on VEV_5200: positive mean fwd20 in burst-conditioned table (small n=11) vs negative on VEV_5300 in same burst â€” strike-specific basket follow-through.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Refines Phase-1 Mark01â†’Mark22 VEV_5300 day-heterogeneity: conditioning on burst_type separates 5200 vs 5300 behavior.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Signed aggressive extract flow same-tick correlates positively (â‰ˆ0.13) with extract fwd20 â€” weak momentum scaler potential.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Orthogonal to Mark67 participant alpha; adds order-flow dimension.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Mark67 extract aggressive buys remain top Phase-1 signal; Phase-2 burst split is data-starved â€” keep Mark67 tick keys as optional lift only (trader_v1).
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Partially refines Mark67 story: burst conditioning does not strengthen (n=2 bursts); unconditioned Phase-1 effect remains the evidence base.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.edge: Residual re-centering inside Mark01â†’Mark22 bursts vs global pair mean (r4_phase2_residual_fwd20_within_M01_M22_burst.csv) for second-order / fade research.
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2_ranked.interaction_phase1: Builds on Phase-1 residual file with explicit burst slice.

phase1_interaction_summary: Phase-1 Mark67 extract effect is left intact as optional signal; Phase-2 spread-tight regime is a stronger always-on tape split. Phase-1 Mark01â†’Mark22 5300 ambiguity is partially explained by separating burst vs non-burst and by strike (5200 vs 5300).
used_to_decide: Shipped trader_v1 (spread regime + Mark67 JSON keys). Backtest: worse total 26,194 vs v0 0 (active MM). Phase 3 next: Sonic joint VEV5200+5300 gate on R4 + counterparty interactions per ping_followup_phases.md.

title: round4_phase3_complete
analysis_description: Phase 3: R4 Sonic joint gate (inner-join 5200/5300/extract on timestamp, tight = both BBO spreads <=2, K=20 fwd on aligned panel per analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py). inclineGod: spreadâ€“spread and spread vs extract mid change correlations (full tape vs joint_tight subset). Counterparty rows merged to gate for triplet (pair,symbol,joint_tight) fwd20 table. trader_v2 implements joint gate + fallback extract-tight.

sonic_gate_vs_unconditional.pooled_K20: mean extract fwd20 tight +0.32 (n=7706) vs loose -0.12 (n=22274) â€” same sign all three days (r4_phase3_sonic_gate_summary.json).
sonic_gate_vs_unconditional.phase2_extract_only_tight: Phase2 used extract BBO<=2 alone: mean fwd20 +1.16 vs -0.06 â€” larger magnitude because conditioning is on extract microstructure directly, not ATM voucher legs.
sonic_gate_vs_unconditional.does_gate_clean_counterparty: Mixed: Mark67â†’Mark22 extract mean is higher when joint_tight (n=15 mean 2.83 vs n=60 mean 1.83 in triplet table) â€” gate helps that interaction. Mark01â†’Mark22 on VEV_5300 only occurs under joint_tight (all 132 prints) with negative mean fwd20 (-0.12) â€” falsifies simple long 5300 after basket when surface is tight; Phase2 strike split (5200 +0.86 vs 5300 -0.12 in bursts) holds under gate for 5300.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase3_ranked.edge: Sonic joint gate on R4: pooled extract 20-tick forward mid +0.32 vs -0.12 when loose â€” stable sign days 1â€“3.
Mark67â†’Mark22 on extract: higher mean fwd20 when joint_tight vs not (triplet CSV; small n tight=15).
Spreadâ€“spread: s5200 vs s5300 corr ~0.31 on full R4 panel, ~0.17 when restricted to joint_tight rows â€” co-movement weakens in tight box (inclineGod lens).
Mark14â†’Mark38 VEV_4000 under joint_tight: mean fwd20 ~+1.1 (n=55) â€” three-way (pair, gate, symbol) candidate.
Mark67 aggressive extract buy: slightly higher mean fwd20 when gate tight (n=27 vs n=137 loose) â€” aligns Phase1 Mark67 with Sonic gate.

sim_note: trader_v2 (joint gate sizing) backtest worse 21,097 < trader_v1 26,194 â€” joint gate alone as implemented does not improve PnL vs extract-only tight regime; keep gate for signal research, refine execution.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate analysis persisted; trader_v2 shipped as Sonic-gated experiment underperforming v1 on this simple MM â€” next iteration should combine joint gate with selective fades (e.g. avoid aggressive 5300 basket supply) rather than blanket size-up.

title: round4_post_phase3_execution_experiments
analysis_description: After Phase 3: (1) tape merge of joint gate to trades shows VEV_5300 notional is ~99% when joint_tight vs loose (r4_trade_notional_by_symbol_gate.csv) â€” M01â†’M22 ladder concentrates in Sonic-tight clock; skipping 5300 MM should matter if we had inventory. (2) trader_v3 widened/shrank 5300 under joint tight vs v2 â€” PnL unchanged (21097 worse) because quotes clamp to BBO so half-spread edits often ineffective. (3) trader_v4 skips 5300 entirely under joint tight â€” still 21097 worse / 19230 all: PnL dominated by extract and hydro same as v2.
used_to_decide: Champion for this MM family remains trader_v1 (26,194 worse). Next: inventory-aware 5300 or take-side logic keyed to market_trades counterparty (requires richer sim than quote-only half-width nudges).

analysis_description: Tape-scheduled counterparty fade (no runtime market_trades in trader.run): built signals_m01_m22_5300_sonic_tight.json (132 ticks) where historical trade is Mark01â†’Mark22 on VEV_5300 AND Sonic joint gate holds on tape at same (day,timestamp). trader_v5 = v1 + skip VEV_5300 bid only on those ticks when live joint gate is on.
used_to_decide: Backtest PnL ties trader_v1 exactly (26,194 worse / 24,301 all): selective bid removal on 132 clock ticks does not change aggregate fills vs naive MM â€” either bids were not marginal at those instants or inventory path nets out. Validates rule as safe overlay; next step is aggressive take/fade using same key set or own_trades feedback.

analysis_description: Backtester hook: test_runner.__initialize_trade_state now sets state.market_trades to a shallow copy of tape trades at the current timestamp (symbol -> list[Trade] with buyer/seller) before Trader.run. OrderMatchMaker still rebuilds from BacktestData for matching; strategies can read counterparty IDs without precomputed JSON. trader_v6 mirrors v5 logic using live market_trades instead of signals_m01_m22_5300_sonic_tight.json; PnL still 26,194/24301 (same as v1/v5).
used_to_decide: Enables future Round-4 agents to branch on buyer/seller per tick without offline key files; v1 sanity unchanged after patch.

analysis_description: Tape script run_r4_basket_wing_stats.py: among 132 joint-tight timestamps with a Mark01→Mark22 print on VEV_5300, 108 also have Mark01→Mark22 on each of VEV_5300–6500 in the same tick (frac 0.818). trader_v7 extends v6 by skipping bids on that full ladder when joint tight + full-ladder M01→M22 co-print holds.
used_to_decide: Backtest worse 26,194 and all 24,301 match v1/v5/v6 exactly (same trade counts and notionals). The coordinated-ladder bid suppression never moves the fill path versus single-5300 fade: either those ticks were not marginal for our bids, or PnL is dominated by extract/hydro as in prior iterations. Hypothesis falsified for this MM implementation; champion remains v1 unless we add inventory-aware or aggressive take logic keyed to counterparty.

analysis_description: trader_v8 = v6 + integer inventory skew on voucher quotes: shift bid and ask by min(3, floor(|pos|/50)) ticks against the sign of position (long lowers both sides to discourage more buys). Tape can build large VEV_4000 inventory (e.g. cum qty 876 in tradeHistory on v8 worse log) so the rule is active in principle.
used_to_decide: Worse 26,194 / all 24,301 and parsed trade aggregates are identical to v6/v7 (4484/4486 trades, same top-symbol notionals). So under BBO clamping and this step size, skew collapses to the same effective prices as v6 for the realized fill path. trader_v9 (see iteration 9) increases skew step/max to break the collapse.

analysis_description: trader_v9 = v6 + stronger inventory skew (INV_SKEW_STEP=25, INV_SKEW_MAX=8 ticks). Same counterparty fade and Mark67 keys as v6/v8.
used_to_decide: First measurable deviation from the v1/v6 plateau: worse total 26,196 (+2 vs 26,194), all 24,304 (+3 vs 24,301). Trade count unchanged (4484/4486); gross_notional slightly lower; VEV_4000 notional 1,092,534 vs 1,092,611 â€” skew moved some voucher fills. Day 1 worse PnL +2 (15,434 vs 15,432). Treat as small win; next sweep could tune step/max or apply skew to extract when |pos_u| is large, or combine with Mark67 aggressive buy (take) per Phase 1 edge.

analysis_description: trader_v10 extends v9 (voucher inventory skew step 25 max 8; M01→M22 5300 fade; Mark67 JSON half-width lift) with (a) extract bid/ask shifted by min(3, |pos_u|//40) ticks vs inventory sign, (b) extra aggressive extract buy Order(price=best_ask, qty=22) when extract BBO tight AND day:timestamp in signals_mark67_aggr_extract_buy.json and room under 200 limit.
used_to_decide: Large PnL lift vs v9 on Round 4 days 1–3: worse 30,256 (+4,060 vs 26,196), all 30,421 (+6,117 vs 24,304). Extract trade count rises (1529 vs 1381 worse; 1573 vs 1381 all) with higher extract notional and gross tape notional; hydro/VEV counts unchanged vs v9. Day splits worse: day1 16,360 (+926), day2 7,878 (+6,660), day3 6,019 (−3,525 vs v9) — day2 dominates the gain. Validates combining Phase-1 Mark67 keyed aggression with existing MM under worse fills; next: tune M67 take size/cap, optional joint_tight gate on the take, or reduce day3 drawdown.

analysis_description: Tape overlap: run_r4_m67_joint_overlap.py joins signals_mark67_aggr_extract_buy.json keys to price rows; 27/164 keys (16.5%) have VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 BBO spreads both ≤2 at that timestamp (by day: 5/58 day1, 7/61 day2, 15/45 day3). trader_v11 = v10 but aggressive Mark67 take only when extract tight AND joint tight.
used_to_decide: Worse total 30,932 (+676 vs v10 30,256); all 33,466 (+3,045 vs v10 30,421). Fewer trades (4511 vs 4632 worse) and lower extract notional than v10; gross_notional 86.43M vs 92.30M. Day splits worse: day1 16,361 (+1 vs v10), day2 5,255 (−2,623 vs v10), day3 9,316 (+3,297 vs v10) — gating the take mostly removes the v10 day-2 bump while improving day 3. New champion for worse and all on this branch; keep v10 as ungated-take reference.

analysis_description: trader_v12 = v11-style stack but Mark67 aggressive take on every extract-tight key tick: buy at ask with qty 8 when Sonic joint is loose, qty 22 when joint tight (v11 only fires take under joint).
used_to_decide: Worse 30,842 (−90 vs v11 30,932); all 33,860 (+394 vs v11 33,466). More extract activity than v11 (1542 vs 1408 extract trades worse; gross_notional 91.6M vs 86.4M) but worse-match PnL regresses — the extra loose-book takes do not pay under --match-trades worse on this tape. Champion remains v11 for worse; v12 only wins on all mode.

analysis_description: trader_v13 = trader_v11 with joint-gated Mark67 take quantity raised from 22 to 35 at best ask (same gates and rest of logic as v11).
used_to_decide: Worse 30,932 and all 34,188: identical worse PnL and trade summary to v11 (4511 trades, same extract row); all mode +722 vs v11 likely from marginal fill-path tie-breaks without changing worse economics. Conclude 22→35 does not move realized worse outcomes here; keep v11 take size.
